title: History, Objects, References, and Forensic Inspection · Graph Lens: Log Graph Format
summary: Explain when and why a behavior changed using graph, path, object, author, and content evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git log graph format increment in a history-forensics evidence packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Query commit graph topology, decorations, authorship, dates, messages, and changed paths with an intentional log format.
Create a cohesive commit with an imperative summary, rationale, test evidence, and no accidental generated or secret data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

content.text: Query commit graph topology, decorations, authorship, dates, messages, and changed paths with an intentional log format. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: History has commits a11ce00 → b00b1e5 → c0ffee6; a file moved from notes.txt to docs/notes.txt with unchanged bytes. Explain graph, object identity, path evidence, and blame limits. A defensible solution applies Log Graph Format, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a history-forensics evidence packet.

content.code:
git log --graph --decorate --oneline --all
git show --stat --summary HEAD
git cat-file -t HEAD^{tree}
git log --follow -- docs/notes.txt
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
